description:
You are an astrodynamics analyst at a commercial GEO satellite operator. You have been assigned to calculate the annual stationkeeping ΔV budget for the newly assigned AsiaCom-3 satellite at 120°W longitude.

Your task:
1. Read the specification document on the Desktop at `~/Desktop/geosat_assignment.txt`.
2. Create a GMAT script simulating AsiaCom-3 in its initial orbit. Configure the force model exactly as requested in the document (Earth gravity degree/order >= 12, Sun, Moon, and SRP enabled; Drag off).
3. Propagate the orbit for exactly 30 days.
4. Use ReportFile or similar outputs to record the time-history of inclination and longitude.
5. From the 30-day simulation data, extrapolate the annual inclination growth rate (deg/year) and the longitude drift rate (deg/year), and determine the longitude drift direction (Eastward or Westward).
6. Calculate the annual North-South and East-West stationkeeping ΔV (m/s/year) using the formulas in the specification document.
7. Write the final stationkeeping budget report to `~/GMAT_output/stationkeeping_budget.txt` containing EXACTLY these lines:
   INC_growth_rate_degperyear: <value>
   LON_drift_rate_degperyear: <value>
   LON_drift_direction: <Eastward or Westward>
   NS_deltav_msperyear: <value>
   EW_deltav_msperyear: <value>
   total_deltav_msperyear: <value>
8. Save the GMAT script to `~/Documents/missions/stationkeeping.script` and ensure it runs successfully.

Login: username=ga, password=password123
